properties.remark: MOBILE HOME LOST A ROOF IN BISMARCK.
TREES AND POWER LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN AROUND HOT SPRINGS AND ALSO THE NORTHERN PART OF THE COUNTY.
NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. A FENCE WAS BLOWN DOWN AS WELL. THE REPORT IS FROM THE ALUM FORK COOPERATIVE OBSERVING STATION.
*** 1 INJ *** A COUPLE OF LARGE TREES WERE DOWNED...WITH ONE TREE ON A HOUSE. A WOMAN WAS INJURED AT THE HOUSE. A METAL ROOF WAS PEELED BACK AT A NEARBY LUMBER YARD.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR LAKE WINONA...PASSING THROUGH THE OUACHITA NATIONAL FOREST BEFORE LIFTING WEST OF LAKE MAUMELLE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. JUST OFF HIGHWAY 10...ONE BARN WAS DETROYED AND ANOTHER HAD THE ROOF BLOWN OFF. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF1. THE TORNADO BEGAN 6 MILES W OF PARON AND ENDED AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 10 AND HIGGINBOTHAM ROAD IN WESTERN PULASKI COUNTY. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 10.6 MILES.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AROUND BAUXITE.
TREES DOWN ON HIGHWAY 9.
TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN IN THE EASTERN PART OF PERRY COUNTY NEAR WILLIAMS JUNCTION.
TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED NEAR THE SALINE COUNTY LINE...OR AROUND LONSDALE.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 5 AND OWENSVILLE CUTOFF ROAD. SEVERAL TREES FELL ON THE ARKANSAS FORESTRY COMMISSION BUILDING NEAR THE INTERSECTION.
A TREE WAS REPORTED DOWN ON INTERSTATE 530 AT U.S. HIGHWAY 67.
A ROOF WAS PARTIALLY TORN OFF OF A HOUSE.
NUMEROUS TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN.

DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF BEARSKIN LAKE. THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES BLOWN DOWN. LARGE METAL BUILDING HAD TWO DOORS BLOWN IN...WITH ONE SIDE OF THE BUILDING BLOWN OUT. ROOF DAMAGE TO MANY HOUSES. ONE SHED DESTROYED AND ANOTHER HAD ITS ROOF TAKEN OFF. THIS STORM HAS BEEN RATED AN EF1 TORNADO. THE TORNADO BEGAN 2.6 MILES NE OF SCOTT AND ENDED 4.6 MILES NE OF SCOTT. THIS TORNADO HAD A TRACK LENGTH OF 2.3 MILES.
TELEPHONE POLE SNAPPED ON HIGHWAY 13 NEAR MCRAE
WIDESPREAD TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS TOWN. REPORTS OF ROOF TORN OFF ARSENAL.

WIDESPREAD TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS TOWN. REPORTS OF ROOF TORN OFF ARSENAL. INDICATIONS ARE THAT THE DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY AN EF1 TORNADO. THE SURVEY IS STILL ONGOING...AND MORE DETAILS WILL BE RELEASED UPON COMPLETION.
POWER LINES WERE DOWN...WITH POWER OUTAGES IN TOWN.
WATER IS COVERING ROADS IN THE CITY AND STREAMS ARE RISING.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH NEAR HAZEN.
LOCAL COORDINATOR REPORTED HARS CREEK ROAD IS BLOCKED ROM 3 MILES SOUTH OF HWY 56 TO THE STRAWBERRY RIVER.
NUMEROUS ROADS UNDER WATER IN BATESVILLE AND ALONG HWY 167 ALL THE WAY TO CAVE CITY. SOME CULVERTS HAVE BEEN WASHED OUT.
WATER IN HOUSES AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 167 AND WEAVER CHAPEL RD.
POWER LINES DOWN AT THE CARLISLE EXIT OF INTERSTATE 40. WIND DAMAGE MAY BE FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO.
TREES WERE DOWNED AND A ROOF WAS REMOVED FROM A HOUSE AT BRINKLEY. TWO 18 WHEELERS WERE OVERTUNED ON INTERSTATE 40 JUST NORTHEAST OF TOWN.
TREES AND SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN IN TOWN.
TREES WERE DOWNED IN AND AROUND TOWN...WITH POWER OUTAGES REPORTED.
ROAD SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN.
THUNDERSTORM GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 60 MPH WITH PENNY SIZE HAIL.
SPOTTER REPORTED WIND GUSTS OF 60 MPH WITH A HANDHELD ANEMOMETER AS WELL AS HAIL RANGING FROM PEA SIZED TO QUARTER SIZED.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL...MEASURED 56 MPH WIND GUST...0.80 INCHES OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES
HAIL LASTED 3 MINUTES...MAINLY PEA TO MARBLE SIZE

QUARTER SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED AT THE HOT SPRINGS AIRPORT.
PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED AT COLONEL GLEN RD. AND I430.
REPORTED BY LITTLE ROCK POLICE AT BASELINE AND PRODUCTION ROAD.
HAIL LASTED FOR THREE MINUTES AND COVERED THE GROUND. HAIL FOG WAS ALSO PRESENT.
LOCATED AT THE MAUMELLE POLICE DEPARTMENT
SOME TREE BRANCHES WERE BEING KNOCKED DOWN BY THE HAIL.
LASTED FOR TWO TO THREE MINUTES.
LASTED FOR TWO TO THREE MINUTES
LASTED FOR SIX MINUTES AND COVERED THE GROUND
SEVERAL SMALL TREE LIMBS WERE ALSO BEING BLOWN DOWN BY WIND.
MULTIPLE TREES HAVE FALLEN ALONG HWY 5. PEA TO DIME SIZE HAIL IS ALSO FALLING.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN OVER ACROSS ROADS IN THE TOWN.
HALF OF A ROOF WAS RIPPED OFF AND DISPLACED.
SEVERAL POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN
SEVERAL TREES AND POWERLINES HAVE BEEN BLOWN DOWN ALONG HWY 154.

SEVERAL BUSINESSES AND ONE HOME SUSTAINED DAMAGE ON THE NORTH SIDE OF HEBER SPRINGS ALONG HIGHWAY 25. THE ROOF OF ONE BUILDING WAS BLOWN OFF. THE BAY DOORS AT TWO CAR DEALERSHIPS WERE DESTROYED. A POWER POLE AND SOME POWER LINES WERE ALSO KNOCKED DOWN.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG SEVERAL ROADS JUST OUTSIDE OF STELLTOWN.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO CHICKEN HOUSES.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF POWER OUTAGES IN ARKADELPHIA AND COUNTY WIDE. SOME OUTAGES DUE TO EXCESSIVE LIGHTNING WHILE MOST ARE THE RESULT OF STRONG WINDS.
MULTIPLE POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN WITH CITY WIDE POWER OUTAGES IN GURDON. SEVERAL ROADS ARE BLOCKED BY FALLEN TREES. ONE HOME HAS BEEN REPORTED WITH MAJOR DAMAGE. NO INJURIES REPORTED AT THIS TIME.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR WHELEN SPRINGS THEN MOVED EAST OF TOWN BEFORE LIFTING. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. OTHER POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED. ONE HOME HAD SOME SHINGLES AND A TV ANTENNA BLOWN OF THE ROOF...WITH SOME SIDING ALSO TORN OFF. A LARGE TREE FELL AND CHRUSHED A TRACTOR SHED. A CANNING SHED WAS DESTROYED CAUSING MASON JARS TO SCATTER. THE TORNADO BEGAN 0.5 MI NW OF WHELEN SPRINGS AND ENDED 3.6 MI E OF WHELEN SPRINGS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 4 MILES.
ONE LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
ONE LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
ONE LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
ONE LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.

TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.

TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN. LOTS OF STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS WITH SIDING AND AWNINGS TWISTED ON THE GROUND.
MULTIPLE POWERLINES ARE DOWN IN SHERIDAN. REPORTS OF POWERLINES BEING DOWN ARE ALSO BEING REPORTED THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.
POWER LINES AND A LARGE TREE WERE BLOWN DOWN.
ONE LARGE TREE WAS SNAPPED OFF.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ON HIGHWAYS AND ROADS IN RISON.
MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN.
*** 1 INJ *** FLYING TREE LIMB STRUCK A MAN IN THE HEAD AND HE WAS TAKEN TO THE HOSPITAL.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG I530.
THE ROOF OF THE FIRE DEPARTMENT WAS DESTROYED. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. TREES ALONG HWY 54 WERE REPORTED DOWN WITH SOME TREE TOPS SNAPPED.
NUMEROUS TREES AND LIMBS ARE DOWN ACROSS THE TOWN WITH ONE TREE FALLING ON A HOUSE. MULTIPLE LIGHT POLES ACROSS THE TOWN HAVE BEEN CRACKED IN HALF.
A MOBILE HOME WAS FLIPPED ON ITS SIDE ALONG HWY 212. NO INJURIES REPORTED. ROOFS OF SOME HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND A CHICKEN HOUSE HAD THE ROOF TORN OFF.
DEPUTY REPORTS DAMAGE TO HOUSE. SIDING AND SHINGLES WERE TORN OFF BY THE WIND.
MULTIPLE HOMES WITH MINOR DAMAGE AND A FEW WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. POWER OUTAGES THROUGHOUT THE CITY FROM FALLEN POWER LINES.
SEVERAL TREES ARE DOWN IN HAMPTON.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN MONTICELLO.
A TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL ON A VEHICLE. NO INJURY WAS REPORTED.
SPOTTER REPORTED TREES IN THE ROAD AND MINOR DAMAGE TO HOUSES.
SEVERAL TREES HAVE FALLEN ALONG ROADS IN YANCOPIN.
A SHED ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF...TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AND A CARPORT WAS DAMAGED.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...A PORCH WAS BLOWN OFF A HOUSE...AND A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A MOBILE HOME.

THIS IS AN UPDATE OF DAMAGE REPORTED EARLIER 3 MILES NORTH OF SKUNK HOLLOW. A MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND HAD ITS ROOF TORN OFF. THIS MOBILE HOME WAS NEARLY BROADSIDE TO THE WIND AND NEXT TO AN OPEN FIELD. THE RESIDENT WAS NOT HOME AT THE TIME. TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...WITH ONE TREE FALLING ON A HOUSE. FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN. A CARPORT WAS DESTROYED. POWER LINES WERE KNOCKED DOWN. THE ROOF OF A PORCH COLLAPSED. THE DAMAGE WAS ALONG THE SOUTHEAST CITY LIMITS OF CONWAY.
A HAY BARN SUFFERED MAJOR DAMAGE...WITH PARTS OF THE BARN BLOWN WELL OUT INTO A FIELD.
TWO HOUSES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
A POWER POLE AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...CAUSING SEVERE DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME. THERE WERE NO INJURIES.
TREES REPORTED BLOWN DOWN WITH POWER OUTAGES IN CLARENDON.
MULTIPLES TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO CHICKEN HOUSES AND A LARGE TREE WAS REPORTED DOWN IN BOGG SPRINGS.
A HOUSE WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED...WITH WINDOWS BLOWN OUT...AND AWNING RIPPED OFF. IRONWORK IN THE HOUSES YARD WAS BENT. LARGE AND SMALL LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES ARE DOWN ON A HOUSE AND VEHICLE ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF MENA.

A TORNADO DESTROYED A WAREHOUSE USED BY A SNACK FOOD COMPANY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOW DOWN...AND ONE OF THE TREES FELL ON A HOUSE. A BARN WAS DAMAGED AS WELL. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF1...AND TRACKED ONLY HALF A MILE BEFORE DISSIPATING 1.4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MENA.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3.7 MILES SE OF DANVILLE AND TRAVELLED EIGHT TENTHS OF A MILE BEFORE LIFTING 3.6 MILES SE OF DANVILLE. A BARN WAS DESTROYED AND TWO PIECES OF FARMING EQUIPMENT WERE DAMAGED. SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS LOCATED ENTIRELY ON PRIVATE PROPERTY.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN OVER.
A FRITO LAY STORAGE WAREHOUSE WAS DESTROYED. THIS REPORT WAS RELAYED BY THE MEDIA.
POWER LINES AND POWER POLES ARE DOWN ACROSS THE CITY OF MENA. A GOOD PART OF THE CITY IS WITHOUT POWER.
AN EF1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 1 WNW OF CHICKALAH TO 1 ENE SULPHUR SPRINGS...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.8 MILES. A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED...AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TELEPHONE POLES ARE DOWN IN RUSSELLVILLE.
A TRAILER HOUSE WAS BLOWN OVER AND DESTROYED. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN. MULTIPLE HOUSES HAVE DAMAGE TO THEM BUT THE EXTENT OF THIS DAMAGE IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.
TREES ARE DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 27
TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN IN RUSSELLVILLE.
WIND GUST ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75 AND 80 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES ARE DOWN ACROSS THE TOWN.
*** 2 FATAL *** THE TOP OF A TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME...KILLING TWO OCCUPANTS.
TREES AND POWERLINES ARE DOWN. SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED.
HIGH WINDS REMOVED ROOF OFF OF BUILDING IN BATESVILLE. OTHER PARTS OF THE COUNTY HAD SOME TREES BLOWN DOWN BUT NO OTHER PROPERTY DAMAGE AT THIS TIME.
TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON HOUSES ON ESCUADRON LANE AND TREES ON A GAZEBO AND HOUSES ON ENCANTADO LANE

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.9 MILES NORTH OF FOUNTAIN LAKE IN GARLAND COUNTY. IT TRACKED 7.2 MILES NORTHEASTWARD AND LIFTED 9.2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF PARON IN SALINE COUNTY. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...AND SOME OF THEM LANDED ON HOUSES. ONE HOUSE HAD ITS PORCH AND PART OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. THERE WERE A COUPLE OF BARNS WITH ROOF DAMAGE...AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED.
REPORT OF A TREE FALLEN ON A HOUSE WITH ATLEAST ONE PERSON TRAPPED INSIDE.
LARGE TREE FELL DOWN ON ROOF OF HOUSE IN THE OVERVIEW SUBDIVISON.
A LARGE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS BLOWN ACROSS THE FIELD.
SEVERAL BARNS AND HOUSES HAVE SUSTAINED DAMAGE IN THE GLEN ROSE COMMUNITY.
TREES DOWN ACROSS THE CITY AND FAULKNER COUNTY.
TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 167.
WIND GUSTS OF 40 MPH AND SMALL TREE LIMBS HAVE BEEN REPORTED DOWN AS WELL.

AN EF1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 2 MILES EAST OF TRASKWOOD TO 1.5 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF TULL...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 3 MILES. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED...AND A BARN WAS DESTROYED...WITH THE BARN ROOF THROWN OVER A HUNDRED YARDS. SEVERAL HOMES WERE MISSING SHINGLES WITH ONE HOME MISSING NEARLY 50 PERCENT OF THE SHINGLES.
SEVERAL TRRRES HAVE FALLEN.
POWERLINES ARE DOWN AND POWER IS OUT IN PARTS OF MALVERN.
FUNNEL CLOUD SPOTTED HEADING INTO PARON. DEBRIS HAS BEEN REPORTED WITH TREES DOWN.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 MI SW OF SARDIS IN SALINE COUNTY TO 3 MI SSE OF WRIGHTSVILLE IN PULASKI COUNTY WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 16.6 MI. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED. DAMAGE WAS SUSTAINED TO A MOBILE HOME THAT LOST ITS ROOF AND PORCH. SEVERAL HOUSES HAD ROOF DAMAGE FROM FALLING TREES. ROOF AND CHIMNEY OF A HOUSE WAS DAMAGED...COINCIDENTALLY THIS SAME HOUSE ON U.S. HIGHWAY 167 WAS HIT BY A TORNADO ABOUT 30 YEARS AGO. THE ROOF WAS TAKEN OFF OF A COUNTRY CLUB.

*** 2 FATAL *** TORNADO TRACKED FROM JUST SOUTHWEST OF INTERSECTION OF WEST MARKHAM STREET AND JOHN BARROW ROAD TO THE SHERRILL HEIGHTS NEIGHBORHOOD...ENDING ABOVE REBSAMEN PARK. THIS TORNADO OCCURED ENTIRELY WITHIN THE CITY LIMITS OF LITTLE ROCK. WHILE THERE WAS SHINGLE DAMAGE...MUCH OF THE DAMAGE CONSISTED OF TREES FALLING ONTO HOUSES...VEHICLES AND POWER LINES. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 3.9 MILES.
*** 2 FATAL *** A LARGE TREE FELL ONTO A HOUSE...KILLING TWO OCCUPANTS. THIS IS ALSO 6 WEST OF DOWNTOWN LITTLE ROCK...JUST NORTHWEST OF THE BARROW ROAD EXIT OF INTERSTATE 630.
*** 2 FATAL *** A LARGE TREE FELL ONTO A HOUSE...KILLING TWO OCCUPANTS.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO EMPTY CHICKEN HOUSES. ONE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF ALONG WITH ONE BARN ROOF. FARRIS CEMENTERY ROAD IS BLOCKED BY MULTIPLE TREES DOWNED.

AN EF1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 3.5 SOUTHWEST OF SWEET HOME TO 2.2 E OF COLLEGE STATION...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 6.6 MILES. TREES AND POWER POLES WERE DOWNED...WITH AN INDUSTRIAL BUILDING DESTROYED. A ROOF OFF OF ANOTHER INDUSTRIAL BUILDING WAS DAMAGED.
10 LARGE TREES DOWN...MOSTLY PINES AND OAKS.
TRRES AND POWERLINE DOWN ALONG I-530.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 7.4 MI SSW OF SCOTT IN PULASKI CO. AND LIFTED 2.3 MI NW OF KEO IN LONOKE CO. WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 6.9 MI. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND A TRAILER OF AN 18-WHEELER WAS ALSO BLOWN OVER. A COTTON TIN WAS DESTROYED...WITH A 150 YR OLD PECAN FARM RECIEVING SEVERE DAMAGE...NEARLY DESTROYED. AN OCCUPANT OF A MOBILE HOME RECIEVED CRITICAL INJURIES WHEN THEIR MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. SEVERAL OTHER MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED WITH ONE KNOCKED OFF ITS FOUNDATION.
NUMEROUS TREES HAVE FALLEN WITH SEVERAL TREES ON HOUSES. GAS LINE RUPTURES HAVE OCCURED AND TWO HOUSES ARE ON FIRE.
*** 1 INJ *** A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED...AND THE OCCUPANT WAS THROWN FROM IT AND SUSTAINED INJURIES.
A LARGE OAK TREE FELL AND DAMAGED A STEEL CONCRETE WALKWAY. ALSO THERE WAS SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE AREA.
LOCATED IN LAKEWOOD VILLAGE.
EIGHT TO TEN HOMES WITH TREE LIMBS OR TREES ON THEM WITH MINOR DAMAGE. MCCAIN BLVD HAS ABOUT EIGHT TO TEN BUISNESSES WITHOUT POWER.

*** 1 FATAL *** A TREE FELL ON AN RV...KILLING THE OCCUPANT INSIDE.
A MOBLIE HOME WAS REPORTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION INJURING AN OCCUPANT. A 250 GALLON PROPANE TANK WAS RIPPED FROM THE FOUNDATION. ALSO A TREE FELL ON A RESIDENCE OUT ON HIGHWAY 365 SOUTH.

A TORNADO DAMAGED OR DESTROYED 50 LARGE STEEL POWER TRANSMITTER TOWERS...WITH POWER LINES...POWER POLES AND TREES DOWNED AS WELL. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF2...AND HAD A PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO TRACKED 7 MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING 6.3 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHEAST OF LONOKE.
TRRES DOWN AND BLOCKING PARTS OF HWY 161. A TRAILED HAS BEEN DAMAGED AND FIRE AND RESUCE IS ON THE SCENE. THERE ARE SEVERAL TRAPPED OCCUPANTS.

HAIL REPORTED SOUTH OF JASPER ALONG HWY 7.
TREES REPORTED DOWN IN DEER.
TREES REPORTED DOWN ALONG HWY 27 JUST BEFORE THE HWY 16 JUNCTION.
GAS STATION REPORTED THAT TREES ARE DOWN ACROSS ASH FLAT. SEVERAL TREES FELL ON POWER LINES AND DOWNED THEM. SOME RESIDENTS ARE WITHOUT POWER AT THIS TIME.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON POWER LINES NEAR CABOT HIGH SCHOOL OFF LINCOLN AND PINE STREET.
A TREE WAS DOWNED ALONG HIGHWAY 70 NEAR THE PULASKI AND LONOKE COUNTY LINE.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL NEARLY COVERED THE GROUND.
HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL FELL NORTH OF GREENBRIER. RELAYED BY KARK-TV IN LITTLE ROCK.
LARGE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN.
A FEW TREES WERE DOWNED NEAR IONE.
HAIL RANGING FROM THE SIZE OF QUARTERS TO GOLF BALLS WAS FALLING IN WHITE HALL AND COVERING THE GROUND.
SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWN IN BRINKLEY.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF BASEBALLS WAS FALLING.
BROKE THE WIENDHSIELD OF THE SPOTTERS CAR,
QUARTER TO PING PONG SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED IN DE WITT.
HAIL RANGED FROM QUARTERS TO PING PONG SIZED. HEAVY RAIN WITH ALMOST TWO INCHES OF RAIN IN ONE HOUR WAS ALSO REPORTED.
NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL FALLING AT THIS TIME.

IN ARKANSAS COUNTY AN EF0 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED. TRACK STARTED 2.8 MILES WEST OF TICHNOR AND ENDED 1 MILE WNW OF TICHNOR FOR A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 1.9 MILES. THIS TORNADO KNOCKED TREES DOWN...BLEW SHINGLES OFF OF HOUSES...AND PEELED TIN BACK ON A BARN. TIME OF THIS DAMAGE WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
2.25 INCH HAIL WAS SEEN.
GOLF BALL TO TENNIS BALL SIZED HAIL PUT HOLES IN SIDING.
TENNIS BALL SIZED HAIL KNOCKED SKYLIGHTS OUT OF A BUSINESS.
A TREE WAS DOWN ALONG HWY 44.
3.25 INCH HAIL BROKE WINDOWS IN A HOME AND BROKE A TRUCKS WINDSHIELD.
PARTS OF HIGHWAY SEVEN...AS WELL AS PARKWAY AVENUE WERE FLOODED WITH OVER A FOOT OF WATER.
MOST MAJOR INTERSECTIONS IN RUSSELLVILLE CLOSED DUE TO 1-2 FEET OF STANDING WATER. OVER 2 INCHES OF RAIN HAD FALLEN IN THE LAST TWO HOURS.
WATER WAS RUSHING DEEP ENOUGH ON MENA STREET TO FLOOD SOME BUSINESSES.
WATER FLOWING INTO BUSINESSES ON MAIN STREET.
SEVERAL SPOTS ON HIGHWAYS 71 AND 28 ARE FLOODED IN AND AROUND WALDRON. FLOOD WATERS REMOVED SOME ASPHALT FROM ROADWAY ON HIGHWAY 28 WEST JUST NORTH OF WALDRON.
A FEW STREETS IN THE CITY WERE BLOCKED OFF DUE TO HIGH WATER. STARDIS ROAD AND PARTS OF HIGHWAY 64 WERE ALSO FLOODED.
SEVERAL LOW LYING AREAS IN AND AROUND CLINTON WERE FLOODED. THERE WERE ALSO SOME REPORTS OF CREEKS OUT OF THEIR BANKS IN NORTHERN VAN BUREN COUNTY.
ARKANSAS HIGHWAYS 80...27...307...AND 309 WERE FLOODED AS WERE NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS.
EVACUATIONS FROM HOUSES ARE IN PROGRESS. PROPANE TANKS ARE FLOATING AWAY. THE SHERIFFS OFFICE HAS NO REPORTS OF INJURIES AT THIS TIME.
WATER WAS 4 FT DEEP AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 71 AND 378.
OFFICIALS WERE CLOSING HIGHWAY 71 DUE TO THE FOURCHE LAFAVE RIVER CROSSING THE ROAD.
